focus: Dark L — The Velarized [ɫ] at Syllable End
focus_ar: صوت الـ L الغامق — الصوت المفخم في نهاية المقطع
arabic_challenge: English has two distinct versions of the letter 'l': a 'light L' (tongue tip touching the ridge, similar to Arabic ل) used at the START of a syllable, and a 'dark L' [ɫ] used at the END of a syllable, where the back of the tongue raises toward the soft palate, giving it a heavier, almost 'oo'-like quality. Arabic speakers typically use only the light L in all positions, which makes words like 'feel' or 'ball' sound noticeably non-native.

target_sounds: dark L [ɫ] at the end of a syllable/word

isolation.instructions: When 'l' ends a word or syllable, the back of your tongue should rise toward the soft palate while the tip still touches behind your teeth — this gives the sound a heavier, darker quality compared to light L.
isolation.instructions_ar: لما l تكون في نهاية كلمة أو مقطع، مؤخرة لسانك لازم ترتفع لسقف الحلق الرخو مع إن طرف اللسان لسه بيلمس خلف الأسنان — ده بيعطي صوت أثقل وأغمق مقارنة بالـ L الخفيفة.

sentence_practice: I feel cool after the tall school bell rang.
The ball fell well past the full pool.
It's a tall, cool school with a big ball field.
daily_drills.record_this: I feel cool after the tall school bell rang — the ball fell well past the full pool near the tall, cool school.
daily_drills.self_check: Can you feel the BACK of your tongue rising for the final L, giving it a heavier quality than a light L?

target_sounds: contrast: light L (syllable start) vs. dark L (syllable end)

isolation.instructions: Practice words that contain BOTH a light L and a dark L, to feel the physical difference between the two within a single word.
isolation.instructions_ar: تدرب على كلمات فيها الاتنين — L خفيفة وL غامقة — عشان تحس بالفرق الجسدي بين الاتنين جوه كلمة واحدة.
practice: little → LIT (light L start-ish) + -tle (dark L end)

sentence_practice: The little local shop is lovely and likely loyal to customers.
This level of loyal, lovely service is likely local.
A likely local level of little, lovely charm.
daily_drills.record_this: The little local shop is lovely and likely loyal to its customers — this level of service is a likely, local, lovely charm.
daily_drills.self_check: In each word, did you correctly use a LIGHT L at the start and a DARK L at the end, feeling the physical shift within the same word?

isolation.instructions: Dark L also appears before another final consonant in clusters like -lk, -lt, -lp, -lm — the darkness of the L affects how the whole cluster sounds.
isolation.instructions_ar: الـ L الغامقة كمان بتظهر قبل صوت ساكن آخر في نهاية الكلمة زي -lk و-lt و-lp و-lm — غمقان الـ L بيأثر على شكل الصوت الكامل.

sentence_practice: Please help me find the milk and salt.
The film's result was worth it for every adult.
I need help with my belt and this film.
daily_drills.record_this: Please help me find the milk and salt — the film's result was worth it for every adult who needed a belt fix.
daily_drills.self_check: In these consonant clusters, did the dark L quality carry through even with another consonant sound right after it?

target_sounds: dark L in longer, more academic vocabulary

isolation.instructions: Apply dark L correctly in longer L2-level vocabulary words that end or have syllables ending in L.
isolation.instructions_ar: طبّق الـ L الغامقة بشكل صحيح في كلمات أطول من مستوى المفردات في المستوى الثاني اللي بتنتهي أو فيها مقاطع بتنتهي بـ L.

sentence_practice: This is a typical political and cultural issue globally.
The natural, moral, and social effects are typical of this global event.
A cultural, moral debate about political and social issues.
daily_drills.record_this: This is a typical political and cultural issue globally — the natural, moral, and social effects matter for everyone.
daily_drills.self_check: In these longer, more advanced words, did the final dark L hold up consistently?

isolation.instructions: Apply this pattern while delivering an argumentative or analytical statement, matching L2's frequent debate-style speaking missions.
isolation.instructions_ar: طبّق النمط ده وانت بتقدم تصريح جدلي أو تحليلي، بما يتماشى مع مهام الكلام المتكررة على شكل نقاش في المستوى الثاني.
practice: Deliver Day 4's sentences with confident, debate-style emphasis.

pair: debate-style delivery with correct dark L

sentence_practice: This political and cultural issue is a global problem, not just a local one.
The moral and social responsibility falls on all of us globally.
A typical political debate about cultural and social values.
daily_drills.record_this: This political and cultural issue is a global problem — the moral and social responsibility falls on all of us, not just locally.
daily_drills.self_check: At debate speed and with confident delivery, did the dark L quality survive on every relevant word?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: dark L at word-end, light-vs-dark contrast, dark L in clusters, academic vocabulary, debate speed
challenge_sentences: I feel the political and cultural level of this global issue is a moral and social problem.
Help me understand the local, typical result of this cultural, political debate.
The little, local school has a global, cultural, and moral impact on all of us.
daily_drills.record_this: I feel the political and cultural level of this global issue is a moral and social problem — help me understand the local, typical result of this debate.
daily_drills.self_check: This passage has many L sounds in different positions. Did you correctly distinguish light vs. dark L throughout?

test_yourself.instructions: Record this passage naturally, correctly distinguishing light L (syllable-start) from dark L (syllable-end) throughout.
test_yourself.passage: I truly feel that global political and cultural issues deserve a local, practical solution. Little steps, like a lovely local school program, can have a real, natural impact on social and moral values. The level of loyal community support will likely determine the final result — and I believe a full, well-planned effort could help solve this typical, but important, problem.

sounds_to_check.dark L (syllable-end): feel, global, political, cultural, local, level, final, result, full, well

scoring_guide.90_100: Light and dark L correctly and consistently distinguished throughout, even in words containing both.
scoring_guide.70_89: Mostly correct; occasional light-L-only pronunciation on dark-L words.
scoring_guide.50_69: Dark L present only in drilled, isolated words.
scoring_guide.below_50: Light L used throughout regardless of position — go back and repeat Days 1-4, focusing on the tongue-back-raising motion.

for_arabic_speakers: This is Blueprint Table 19's explicit L2 Week 5 assignment (Dark L).

A helpful trick: try saying an 'oo' sound right before the L at the end of a word (fee-OOL for 'feel') to feel the tongue-back-raising motion, then gradually reduce the exaggerated 'oo' until it's just a natural dark L quality.
This sound, once mastered, immediately makes words like 'cool', 'well', 'girl', and 'world' — extremely common English words — sound significantly more native.
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record the 'record_this' sentence every day. By Day 7, compare with your L0/L1 recordings of similar words — this should sound like a clear accent upgrade.
